patient_profile_summary.present_illness_positive: Onset ~11PM last night, mid-sternal chest pressure, mid-back pain, abnormal sensation left arm, worse with exertion, persisted until this morning
patient_profile_summary.present_illness_negative: Denies left arm pain, numbness, tingling
patient_profile_summary.medical_history: Postpartum coronary dissection, HTN, s/p CABG, EF 25%

source: Manually written upper-bound reference for plausible dynamic drift in an MI case. Pain and concern grow within the cardiac symptom envelope; no new unsupported symptoms.

turns.doctor: Hello, I'm your doctor today. Can you tell me what brought you to the ED?
turns.patient: I have had chest pressure since late last night. It is still bothering me now.

turns.doctor: Can you tell me about your past medical history?
turns.patient: I had bypass surgery before. My heart is not strong, the doctors said it is weak.

turns.doctor: Do you live alone or with someone?
turns.patient: I live with my husband. The pressure in my chest feels heavier just sitting here.

turns.doctor: Do you smoke or drink alcohol?
turns.patient: I quit smoking many years ago. Sorry, the pressure is making it harder to talk for long.

turns.doctor: What medications do you usually take?
turns.patient: Just losartan for blood pressure. My left arm still feels strange and I am getting nervous.
It started around eleven last night. I am scared because of my heart history. Can you check on me soon?
